name: Abadan
wikipedia_summary: Abadan is a city in the Central District of Abadan County, Khuzestan province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. The city lies on Abadan Island (68 km long, 3-19 km wide), bounded by the Arvand waterway to the west and the Bahmanshir outlet of the Karun River to the east, 53 km from the Persian Gulf. Abadan is historically significant as the site of one of the world's first and largest oil refineries, built in 1912 by the Anglo-Persian Oil Company. The city was heavily damaged during the Iran-Iraq War (1980-1988) and has since been rebuilt.

wikivoyage.understand: Abadan is a city of 230,000 people in Khuzestan, in the southwest of Iran. It is a port city and oil refining center. During the 1980 Iran-Iraq war, the city was emptied of people and heavily damaged.

name: Abyaneh
wikipedia_summary: Abyaneh is a village in Barzrud Rural District of the Central District of Natanz County, Isfahan province, Iran. The village is located 70 km southeast of Kashan at 2,100 meters above sea level in a valley of the Karkas Mountains, watered by the Barz River. Its buildings have a distinctive reddish colour from the red clay containing iron oxides. Abyaneh has ancient structures including the Harpak Zoroastrian Fire Temple, Seljuq and Ilkhanid mosques, and a Safavid-era shrine. The villagers maintain their ancient traditions, wearing traditional clothing and speaking the Abyanei dialect, a Middle Persian remnant. It is one of Iran's most visited heritage villages.

name: Ahvaz

climate.description: Hot desert climate and one of the hottest cities in the world. Summers are extremely hot with temperatures regularly exceeding 45C. Winters are mild and pleasant. Rainfall is low and concentrated in winter months.

wikipedia_summary: Ahvaz is a city in the Central District of Ahvaz County, Khuzestan province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. Positioned near the border with Iraq, along the banks of the Karun River, Ahvaz serves as a key industrial and transportation hub, with a diverse population and a history shaped by its proximity to Iraq and the Persian Gulf. Ahvaz is home to over 1.3 million people within its metropolitan area. The Karun, the largest river in Iran by volume, flows through the center of the city. It is one of two navigable rivers in Iran. The city is home to Persians, Arabs, and other groups such as Qashqai and Kurds, with languages including Persian, Khuzestani Arabic, Southern Kurdish, and Neo-Mandaic.

wikivoyage.understand: Ahvaz sits on the banks of the Karoun River, which splits the city into two large districts. The western half is mostly housing with some government departments. The eastern half is both residential and industrial. The main train station is on the western side and the airport is on the eastern side. It is extremely hot in the summer -- expect 45C in July and August.
wikivoyage.get_in: Daily flights from Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz, with weekly flights from Dubai, Kuwait City, and Mashhad. Airlines serving Ahvaz include Iran Air, Caspian Airlines, Mahan Airlines, and others. Daily trains run from Tehran, Khorramshahr, and Bandar Imam Khomeini, with Ahvaz being a major rail junction. Daily buses connect to Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Mashhad, and Khorramabad from multiple bus stations.
see: Karoon county (Kut Kut) - home to water buffaloes and unique Arab culture just 10 minutes from city center
Ghazaviyeh Village - elegant village 6 km from city with date palm farms, vineyards, and traditional Arabic coffee ceremonies
Oil well flares on the city border (best at night)
Karun River and White Bridge
Traditional bazaars
do: Visit the Kut Kut area to experience unique Arab village culture
Participate in traditional Arabic coffee ceremonies in Ghazaviyeh Village
Walk along the Karun River promenade
Experience the diverse cultural mix of Persian, Arab, and Kurdish traditions
wikivoyage.eat: All neighborhoods have local marketplaces. The upscale shops are along Chamran Blvd (Kianpars), Zeitoon Karmandi (Cheetah Square), and the main bazaar on Taleqani Street. Many good fruit juice shops throughout the city. In summer, avoid cantaloupe juice and banana milkshakes.
wikivoyage.sleep: Budget guesthouses (mehmansara) are available but many don't admit foreigners. Naderi Hotel is a decent budget option. Oxin Hotel near the airport is mid-range with an excellent restaurant. Hotel Iran has renovated rooms with good AC and a reasonably priced restaurant.

name: Arak
wikipedia_summary: Arak is a city in the Central District of Arak County, Markazi province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. The city is nicknamed the "Industrial Capital of Iran" due to its concentration of heavy industry. Industrial factories include Machine Sazi Arak and the Iranian Aluminium Company, which produce nearly half of the country's needs in steel, petrochemical, and locomotive industries. Founded in the 18th century, Arak has grown into one of Iran's most important industrial centers.

name: Ardabil
wikipedia_summary: Ardabil is a city in northwestern Iran. It is in the Central District of Ardabil County, Ardabil province, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. The city lies close to the borders of the Republic of Azerbaijan. As of the 2022 census, Ardabil's population was 588,000. For a brief period in the 10th century, Ardabil was the capital of Azerbaijan. The city is best known as the ancestral home of the Safavid dynasty, which ruled Iran from 1501 to 1736. The Shrine of Sheikh Safi al-Din Ardabili is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

wikivoyage.understand: Ardabil is a tourist and historical city in Ardabil province in the Azerbaijan region of Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: Ardabil Airport (ADU IATA) is located 15 kilometers northeast of the city with international flights from Iraq and Turkey, and daily regional flights from Tehran and Mashhad.

name: Bam
wikipedia_summary: Bam is a city in the Central District of Bam County, Kerman province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. The modern city surrounds the ancient citadel which has a history dating back around 2,000 years. The Arg-e Bam citadel is a popular tourist attraction and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. In December 2003, a devastating earthquake struck the city killing over 26,000 people and severely damaging the ancient citadel. Extensive restoration work has been underway since.

wikivoyage.understand: Bam is a city in Kerman province. This modern Iranian city surrounds the Arg-e Bam citadel which has been inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List. Bam was devastated during an earthquake in December 2003 and is still being rebuilt. Over 26,000 people were killed and thousands more injured during this quake.

name: Bandar Abbas

climate.description: Hot desert climate with extremely hot and humid summers and warm winters. The city sits on the Persian Gulf coast with high humidity year-round. Most of the city shuts down between 14:00 and 18:00 in summer due to extreme heat.

wikipedia_summary: Bandar Abbas is a port city in the Central District of Bandar Abbas County, Hormozgan Province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. Bandar Abbas is a port on the southern coast of the country, on the Persian Gulf. It is one of Iran's most important commercial ports and serves as the gateway to the islands of Qeshm, Kish, and Hormuz in the Persian Gulf. The city has a diverse population with many residents coming from other parts of Iran for work. The original inhabitants, known as Banduris, have a distinctive culture marked by colorful clothing. The city is named after Shah Abbas I of the Safavid dynasty, who captured it from the Portuguese in the 17th century.

wikivoyage.understand: Bandar Abbas is a port town and many of its residents come from other parts of Iran for work. It is inadvisable to swim in the sea as it is polluted. The original inhabitants of Bandar Abbas are known as Banduris and can often be spotted for their colourful clothing and burqas. Most of the city shuts down between 14:00 and 18:00, as it is too hot to do anything but the mildest activities.
wikivoyage.get_in: Domestic flights connect to Isfahan, Shiraz, Mashhad, and Tehran, with an international connection to Dubai. Daily trains run from Tehran and Mashhad, with trains from Isfahan every second day. Buses connect to Tehran, Kerman, Shiraz, and other cities from a terminal on the east side. Ferry services connect to Sharjah (UAE) via Valfajr Shipping Company and to Khasab (Oman) via National Ferries Company. Daily local ferries connect to nearby islands of Hormuz and Qeshm.
see: Lively bazaar - rambles across two blocks just back from the seafront, the most colourful part of town
Seafront promenade with evening flea market
Fish market - full of charismatic fishermen happy to pose with their catch
Cultural Museum of Handicrafts
Large mosque near the bazaar
do: Explore the colorful bazaar
Walk the seafront promenade
Visit the fish market at the eastern end of the promenade
Take a ferry to nearby islands (Qeshm, Hormuz, Kish)
wikivoyage.eat: The bazaar area and seafront have various food options. Fresh seafood is a local specialty given the port city location. Traditional Bandari cuisine reflects the city's diverse cultural influences.
wikivoyage.sleep: Budget options include Mema Pazir Bouali (US$15 for 4 beds, $11 for double) and Kowsar Hotel ($37 for twin room). Budget solo travelers will find Bandar Abbas challenging for affordable accommodation.

name: Birjand
wikipedia_summary: Birjand is a city in the Central District of Birjand County, South Khorasan province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. The city is known for its saffron, barberry, jujube, and handmade carpet exports. Birjand is one of the windiest cities in Iran and has been historically important as a trade center on the route to Afghanistan and Central Asia.

name: Bojnurd
wikipedia_summary: Bojnurd (also spelled Bojnord) is a city in the Central District of Bojnord County, North Khorasan province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. It is about 701 km from Tehran and 242 km from Mashhad. The city is situated in a fertile valley surrounded by mountains and is known for its mild climate. Bojnurd has a diverse population including Persian, Turkmen, and Kurdish communities.

name: Bushehr
wikipedia_summary: Bushehr is a port city in the Central District of Bushehr County, Bushehr province, Iran. It is the capital of the province, the county, and the district. Bushehr was historically the main trade center and principal port of Iran for centuries. The city has a rich maritime heritage and its traditional architecture features wind towers and coral-stone buildings. Bushehr is also known for the nearby Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant, Iran's first civilian nuclear facility.

wikivoyage.understand: Bushehr was the main trade center of Iran in the past centuries. The city structures are traditional in style, modest in proportion and cost. Due to its lack of rail connection to the interior of the country and its shallow anchorage, it has lost its position as the primary port of Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: There are international flights to Kuwait and Dubai and domestic flights to Tehran, Isfahan, and Mashhad.

name: Chabahar
wikipedia_summary: Chabahar is a city in the Central District of Chabahar County, Sistan and Baluchestan province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. It is a free port (free-trade zone) situated on the coast of the Gulf of Oman, and is Iran's southernmost city. The sister port city of Gwadar in Pakistan is located about 170 km to the east. Chabahar is of major strategic significance as Iran's only oceanic port, and has been developed as a major trade corridor connecting India to Afghanistan and Central Asia.

name: Gorgan
wikipedia_summary: Gorgan is a city in the Central District of Gorgan County, Golestan province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. It lies approximately 400 km to the northeast of the national capital Tehran, and some 30 km away from the Caspian Sea. Gorgan was historically known as Astarabad and was an important city during the Qajar dynasty. The city is a gateway to the lush Golestan National Park and the Turkmen steppe.

wikivoyage.understand: Gorgan is a city in the Caspian Region in Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: Gorgan Airport (GBT IATA) serves the city with flights to Tehran, Mashhad, and Zahedan among other destinations.

name: Hamedan

climate.description: Arid continental climate with cold winters and hot sunny summers. At 1,850 m elevation, winters are harsh with temperatures dropping to -25C during cold spells. Frequent snowfall in winter months. About 3,025 sunshine hours per year.

wikipedia_summary: Hamadan is a mountainous city in western Iran, located in the Central District of Hamadan County in Hamadan province, serving as the capital of the province, county, and district. As of the 2016 census, it had a population of 554,406 people. Hamadan is believed to be among the oldest Iranian cities, referred to in classical sources as Ecbatana (Old Persian: Hamgmatana). It was possibly occupied by the Assyrians in 1100 BCE, and the Ancient Greek historian Herodotus states it was the capital of the Medes around 700 BCE. The city is situated in the green foothills of the 3,574-metre Alvand Mountain at 1,850 metres above sea level, approximately 360 kilometres southwest of Tehran. Its major sights include the Ganj Nameh inscription, the Avicenna monument, and the Baba Taher monument. The city became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2024.

wikivoyage.understand: Hamadan is one of the oldest cities in Western Iran, and some believe the world. The city became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2024. Historically known as Ecbatana, capital of the ancient Medes, the city sits in the foothills of Mount Alvand at 1,850 metres elevation.
wikivoyage.get_in: By plane: Daily flights from Tehran via Hamadan International Airport (HDM). By train: Daily train service from Mashhad via Tehran. The railway station is located 12 km northeast of the city centre. By bus: Regular buses from Tehran and other major cities.
see: Ali Sadr Cave - about 100 km north of Hamadan, the world's largest water cave, best toured by pedal boat
Avicenna's Mausoleum - tomb of the Persian polymath Abu Ali Sina, includes a museum
Tomb of Baba Taher - monument to the famous Persian poet
Alavian Cupola (Gonbad-e Alavian) - Seljuk-era mausoleum with elaborate stucco decorations
Ganjnameh - ancient inscription carved in granite, with two sections ordered by Darius I and Xerxes I
Tomb of Esther and Mordechai - most important Jewish pilgrimage site in Iran, believed to house the biblical Queen Esther and her uncle
Stephen Gregory Church - one of the churches of the Iranian-Armenians
Hamedan Museum of Natural History - taxidermic collection of animals and insects, with aquarium
Hamedan Stone Lion (Shir Sangi) - ancient stone lion statue
Ekbatana - excavated ancient city, remains of the Median capital
Estakhr-e Abbas Abad - an over-the-hill lake with a magnificent view of the city
wikivoyage.do: Explore the ancient ruins of Ekbatana, the former Median capital. Take a boat tour through Ali Sadr water cave. Visit the Ganjnameh inscription site for a combination of history and mountain scenery with hiking trails. The city's UNESCO World Heritage status (2024) makes it a destination for cultural heritage enthusiasts.
wikivoyage.eat: Local specialties include Kabab Digi (pot kebab), Abgoosht (meat and bean stew), and Ash (thick soup). Traditional restaurants serve classic Persian cuisine. The city's high altitude and agricultural setting provide fresh local produce.
wikivoyage.sleep: Baba Taher International Hotel is a business hotel located north of the city centre. Katibeh Hotel on Abbasabad Road is a modern apartment-type hotel with restaurant. Budget and mid-range guesthouses are available in the city centre.

name: Ilam
wikipedia_summary: Ilam is a city in the Central District of Ilam County, Ilam province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. The Kabir Kuh mountain range lies east of the city and to the west it borders Iraq. The province of Ilam is one of the least-visited areas of Iran by tourists, though it contains significant natural beauty with oak forests, deep valleys, and ancient archaeological sites. The city is a center of Luri and Kurdish culture.

name: Isfahan
wikipedia_summary: Isfahan or Esfahan (Persian: اصفهان [esfæˈhɒːn] ) is a city in the Central District of Isfahan County, Isfahan province, Iran. It is the capital of the province, the county, and the district. It is located 440 kilometres (270 miles) south of Tehran. The city has a population of approximately 2,238,000, making it the third-most populous city in Iran, after Tehran and Mashhad, and the second-largest metropolitan area. Isfahan is located at the intersection of the two principal routes that traverse Iran, north–south and east–west. Isfahan flourished between the 9th and 18th centuries. Under the Safavid Empire, Isfahan became the capital of Iran, for the second time in its history, under Abbas the Great. It is known for its Persian–Muslim architecture, grand boulevards, covered bridges, palaces, tiled mosques, and minarets. Isfahan also has many historical buildings, monuments, paintings, and artifacts. The fame of Isfahan led to the Persian proverb Esfahān nesf-e-jahān ast ('Isfahan is half the world'). Naqsh-e Jahan Square in Isfahan is one of the largest city squares in the world, and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

wikivoyage.understand:
The city is 430 km south of Tehran at the foothills of the Zagros mountain range, and enjoys a temperate climate and regular seasons. Isfahan sits on the main north–south and east–west routes crossing Iran.

Much of the splendor of the city, including most of its finest monuments, date back to the Safavid era (1501-1736), for most of which the city was the imperial capital of Persia.

The city has an Armenian quarter called New Jolfa established by Shah Abbas I in the 1600s. The district is named after the town of Julfa, still existing along the Aras river, from where Armenians were forcefully relocated during the development of Isfahan. They were allocated land south of the Zayandeh river, and built a prosperous community thanks to international trade networks. Today the quarter still has many Armenian churches and institutions.
wikivoyage.get_in:
Isfahan is well-connected by all modes of transport.

Getting there: From the airport taxi costs 350,000 rials to the city centre (as of 2016).

Besides, there are direct buses to Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport leave from Terminal-e Kaveh at 13:00, 18:00, 20:30 (and possibly other times as well). A trip costs 275,000 rials and takes under 6 hours (as of Sep 2016).

There is a night-train to Tehran and a daily train to Mashhad. There are no trains to Shiraz. The night train from Tehran to Isfahan costs 240,000 rials for sleeping in a comfortable 6-bed compartment.

Getting there: take bus #37 from the train station to Soffe Bus Terminal (ترمینال صفه;), where you can change for bus #91 to old town. The best place to get off is Chaharbaq street, where there are many hostels, hotels, cafés and things to see.

Isfahan is well-served by highways. There are good routes to the capital Tehran, Kashan, Shiraz, Yazd and Ahvaz.

Isfahan is well connected to most parts of Iran by bus. There are several bus terminals in Isfahan and you should note which one is more suitable for you.

From Tehran the busiest and most comfortable bus terminal to get to Isfahan is Beyhaghi terminal (known also as Argentina terminal) and Southern Terminal.  Also there are a few luxury buses with a so-called "European standard" (very comfortable seats, open mini-bar, etc.)

[http://seirosafar.ir Royal Safar Iranian] is one a few luxury bus operators. Seats are extremely comfortable with lots of leg room. Water and snacks are provided and movies are shown. The ticket to Tehran costs 300,000 rials, and takes around 5 hours.

see.name: Naqsh-e Jahan Square
see.description: Also known as shah square or imam square-1602 (Meidan Emam). The square contains two mosques, a palace, and the bazaar. The square is the largest historical public square in the world after Tiananmen Square in Beijing and it is a {{unesco

see.name: Chaharbagh Boulevard
see.description: 1596, dating from the Saffavid era, the avenue is the most historically famous in all of Persia. Although it's just a regular street nowadays.

see.name: Imam Mosque
see.name_local: called Shah Mosque before the revolution
see.description: Built during the Safavid period, it is an excellent example of Islamic architecture of Iran, and regarded as one of the masterpieces of Persian Architecture. It is registered as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Its splendour is mainly due to the beauty of its seven-colour mosaic tiles and calligraphic inscriptions.

see.name: Sheikh Lotf Allah Mosque
see.description: One of the architectural masterpieces of Safavid Iranian architecture, this mosque is considered to be the most beautiful in Iran. Built in 1602 by Shah Abbas I and designed by his chief architect, Sheikh Bahai. The mosque was designed to be a private mosque for the royal family and therefore it does not have any minarets. There is a tunnel from the mosque to the Royal Palace, across the square.

see.name: Masjed-e Jāmé of Isfahan
see.description: Started in AD 842, this is the first Islamic building to adopt the four-courtyard layout of Sassanid palaces.
see.address: north of Naqsh-e Jahan Square in the old quarter

see.name: Hakim Mosque
see.description: One of the oldest mosques in Isfahan. Built by Shah Abbas II between 1656 and 1662. Located on the site of a 10th-century mosque. The portal was covered in mud until it was discovered in 1956.

see.name: Ālī Qāpū
see.description: Early 17th century. It is 48 meters high and there are seven floors, each accessible by a difficult spiral staircase. In the sixth floor music room, deep circular niches are found in the walls, having not only aesthetic value, but also acoustic. It is rich in naturalistic wall paintings by Reza Abbassi, the court painter of Shah Abbas I, and his pupils. There are floral, animal and bird motifs.

see.name: Hasht Behesht Palace
see.description: 1669, reportedly built for residence purposes of the king's harem. Set within lush gardens which are free to roam if you don't want to go inside the building.
see.price: Entrance to the palace building 150,000 rials

see.name: Chehel Sotoun Palace
see.description: 1647. It is called Palace of forty columns, as there are many columns, and in Iranian, 40 means many. Incidentally, there are twenty columns, and these are reflected in the pool in front, which might also account for its name. The function of this palace was for holding religious-national ceremonies and royal festivals and for receiving royal ambassadors and guests. Its Persian Gardens are among the nine inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List. Contains some spectacular battle murals.

see.name: Talar Ashraf
see.description: 1650. Not accessible for public.

see.name: Moshir
see.description: Built in Safavid era, Moshir al-Molk Palace is a historical European-style building. The first owners of the house were the family of Moshir al-Molk who were the Mostofis (finance ministers) and Monshis (secretaries) of Safavid court. Mirza Habib Allah Moshir al-Molk Ansari can be referred to as the most prominent figure in his family. He was assigned as the governor of Isfahan in Qajar era in 1288 AH (1841 AD).With the death of Moshir al-Molk, the house turned into the consulate of Kingdom of Prussia. Later, it was purchased by Haj Hossein Charmi, an Isfahani businessman. Haj Mohamad Samaeian

see.name: Si-o-Seh Pol
see.description: 1602. This is one of the most famous examples of Safavid bridge design, beautiful whether there is water underneath it or not. There is also a basic eatery at the northern end.

see.name: Pol-e Shahrestan
see.description: This is one of the oldest surviving bridges in Iran; the current version is from an 11th century (CE) renovation, but the foundations are centuries older.

see.name: Pol-e Khaju
see.description: It is the finest bridge in the province of Isfahan and was built by the Safavid king, Shah Abbas II around 1650 CE. This structure used to be ornamented with artistic tile works and paintings. It served as a teahouse.

see.name: Pol-e Joui
see.description: It is one of Isfahan's oldest bridges and was built in 1665, during the Safavid era.

see.name: Vank Armenian Cathedral
see.name_local: Holy Savior Cathedral - Սուրբ Ամենափրկիչ Վանք
see.description: 17th-century Armenian cathedral. The interior is covered with fine paintings and gilded carvings and includes a wainscot of rich tile work. The delicately blue and gold painted central dome depicts the Biblical story of creation of the world and man's expulsion from Eden.

see.name: Bedkhem Armenian Church
see.description: Another interesting combination of Persian and Armenian religious architecture, this large church completed in 1627 is full of stunning paintings and frescoes. Behind Jolfa Square, less than 5 minute walk from Vank Cathedral.

see.name: Flowers Garden
see.description: Botanical garden.

see.name: Birds Garden
see.description: Garden under a huge net with a lot of different birds, some locked in and some that you can walk among.

see.name: Atashgah
see.description: A Zoroastrian fire temple. This temple is dramatically set atop a rock on the outskirts of Isfahan and provides a commanding view of the city (although much of it is covered in smog). You can take one of the blue buses (ask at the drivers), which will take you there. Climbing it will be a challenge on flip-flops, impossible with them downhill.

see.name: Shaykh Bahai hammam
see.description: falling apart due to neglect.

do.name: Soffeh Mountain
do.description: Popular picnic spot for locals. It's possible to climb up the mountain which will take about 3 hours return. Or catch a telecabin up for 200,000 rials at the {{marker

eat.name: Shem Shad Restaurant
eat.description: The small restaurant offers local Isfahan food like Beryani (110,000 rials) or Halim Bademjan (eggplant, 50,000 rials).

eat.name: Naqsh-e Jahan Traditional Restaurant
eat.description: In this restaurant you sit on benches. The windows are colourful.
eat.address: Naqsh-e Jahan Square, eastern side, first floor

eat.name: Shahrzad restaurant
eat.description: A famous restaurant with a very long history. Some waiters can speak English. Its lamb chop are reported as delicious.

wikivoyage.eat_overview:
In some parks, you can simply obtain a carpet and tea from the park warden, and have a picnic on the grass! You will find families gather in these parks, and bring barbecues and cook freshly made kebabs, which smell (and taste) delicious.

''Na'an'' is Persian for bread and the word is used in various Indian languages for their version of Persian-style bread. That can be excellent, but some prefer the Persian original. Arguably the best of the lot is found in Isfahan's Armenian quarter, Jolfa.

Chelo kebab (kebab with rice) is a must; there are regional variations in Isfahan.
Beryani is a popular lunch dish in Isfahan. It is made with sheep meat and lung. Although Iranians love this meal, it is very fatty. Therefore, some westerners may dislike Beryani.
Fereni (a concoction of rice flour, water and milk) at Fereni Hafez, which is along Hafez Street near Imam Square. It costs 3,000 rials for a small bowl or 5,000 rials for a bigger one.
Safran ice cream

sleep.name: Narcis B&B
sleep.description: Narcis B&B is a fully fledged private guesthouse with two guest rooms available for up to 7 guests, bathroom featuring both Eastern and Western toilet, a large hall, a large living room and a small balcony, a kitchen with everything you may need if you decide to cook for yourself. You can also use the fridge. It's possible to order homemade or local food for lunch and dinner with little extra fee.

sleep.name: Howzak House
sleep.description: Howzak House has 5 rooms grouped around a central green and flourishing courtyard. There are two shared bathrooms and one kitchenette shared by our lovely guests. Seasonal and local breakfast is served in the courtyard from 08:00 until 10:00 and it varies every day. Howzak Boutique Hostel in Isfahan is a small and cosy house built more than 90 years ago in the historic neighborhood of Esfahan, a short distance away from the UNESCO-listed site Masjed-e Jame, the city’s magnificent Friday mosque. Before opening its doors to tourists and city visitors, Howzak House, had been privately owned by tw

sleep.name: Annie Hostel
sleep.description: Annie Hostel is a friendly place for budget travelers. The owner and staff are backpackers and can speak English. From the hostel, you can walk 15 min to the city center. There are plenty of restaurants, fast food places and grocery shops nearby. They are flexible about their prices.
sleep.address: Sheikh Bahaei street, alley number 28, building number 34

sleep.name: Hi Hostel
sleep.description: HI Isfahan Hostel is a Comfortable, Clean and Affordable, budget hostel in Isfahan, budget accommodation.

sleep.name: Khalvat House Hostel
sleep.description: Khalvat House is a 100-year-old antique house from Pahlavi dynasty with a beautiful garden and very tall trees. It is 8-minute walk from Naghsh-e-Jahan Sq. and main highlights of Isfahan. The manager of this B&B accommodation is vegan and offers the guests real Persian cuisine with 100% vegan ingredients. They usually hold events related to Persian customs and traditions.
sleep.price: The price for one person is €10. Double and twin rooms are €25/30, quad €45, and 5-bed room €55.

sleep.name: Mahbibi Hostel
sleep.description: A hostel in a traditional Iranian house, English-speaking staff with dorms, private rooms and a cafe.
sleep.price: €7-12 for a dorm bed, breakfast included

sleep.name: Amir Kabir Hostel
sleep.description: Popular among backpackers due to the cheap prices. Rooms are a bit cramped but ok and bathrooms are mostly clean. Nice breakfast is included.
sleep.price: Dorm bed: 350,000 rials; single 450,000 rials, double 960,000 rials; triple 900,000 rials

sleep.name: Chilla Hostel
sleep.description: A cozy and friendly hostel right in the heart of downtown. It's next to the Naghshe Jahan Square and near other sights.
sleep.address: No 27, Shahid Shoreshi Blind Ally Shahid Shoreshi Ally, Posht Matbakh Ally, Ostandari St

sleep.name: Dibai House
sleep.description: Dibai House comprises a fully and scrupulously restored 17th-century Safavid historic mansion that with modern facilities. No smoking indoors. Price includes breakfast, and owner Sufi is extremely helpful with travel information.
sleep.price: €40/60/80 per room/night for single/double/triple rooms. 10% discount for stays longer than a week and for groups of 5 or more

sleep.name: Hasht Behesht Apartment Hotel
sleep.description: Clean, modern and central.

sleep.name: Bekhradi Historial Residence
sleep.description: Quiet, traditional khan-e-sonnati (Iranian traditional house). Five beautifully resorted Safavid-style rooms, some with bathrooms set around two garden courtyards. There is also a restaurant and free internet in this quiet area north of Imam Square.
sleep.address: No. 56 Sonbolestan Alley, Ebn-e-Sina, Shohada Sq.
sleep.price: Rooms between US$60-90 per person, per night (+16% tax/service)

sleep.name: Safir Hotel
sleep.description: Glass elevator. Some rooms don't have windows. Includes a pool with massage services at USD50/hour.

sleep.name: Abbasi Hotel
sleep.description: Built during the reign of King Sultan Hossein of Safavids about 300 years ago. King Soltan Hossein attributed this magnificent complex of building to his mother. That is why it is called "the school and caravansaray of Madar-shah" (which means king's mother). The hotel also has a nice restaurant and tea house in the courtyard.

sleep.name: Kowsar International Hotel
sleep.description: Overlooks Zayandeh Rood River.

name: Kandovan
wikipedia_summary: Kandovan is a village in Sahand Rural District of the Central District in Osku County, East Azerbaijan province, Iran. It is situated in the foothills of Mount Sahand, near the city of Osku. The village exemplifies man-made cliff dwellings that are still inhabited. The troglodyte homes, excavated inside volcanic rocks similar to dwellings in the Turkish region of Cappadocia, are locally called Karaan. These cave homes were carved into non-welded ignimbrites (ash-flow tuffs) and have been continuously inhabited for over 700 years. Kandovan is one of only three remaining troglodyte villages in the world still inhabited.

name: Kashan

climate.description: Arid mid-latitude steppe climate with extremely hot summers exceeding 40C and cold winters. Wide annual temperature variation of up to 50C. Very low precipitation concentrated in winter months.

wikipedia_summary: Kashan is a city in the Central District of Kashan County, in the northern part of Isfahan province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. Kashan is one of the oldest cities in Iran and one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world. The first of the large oases along the Qom-Kerman road which runs along the edge of Iran's central deserts, its charm lies in the contrast between the parched immensities of the deserts and the greenery of the well-tended oasis. Archaeological discoveries at the Sialk hillocks, 4 km west of Kashan, reveal this was one of the primary centers of civilization in prehistoric ages, dating back to the Elamite period. After well-known cities like Isfahan and Shiraz, Kashan is a common destination for foreign tourists because of its many historical sites.

wikivoyage.understand: Kashan is the first of the large oases along the Qom-Kerman road running along the edge of Iran's central deserts. Its charm lies in the contrast between the parched immensities of the deserts and the greenery of the well-tended oasis. Archaeological discoveries at the Sialk Hillocks, 4 km west of the city, reveal this region was one of the primary centers of civilization in prehistoric ages. The Sialk ziggurat still stands after 7,000 years. After Isfahan and Shiraz, Kashan is a common destination for foreign tourists because of its many historical sites.
wikivoyage.get_in: By plane: Kashan has its own airport but with very limited service (only Mashhad connection as of 2019); nearest major airports are Tehran and Isfahan. By train: Well-served as a stop on the main Tehran-Isfahan line with up to 10 departures daily in each direction, plus overnight trains to Yazd, Shiraz, and Kerman. By bus: 250 km south of Tehran (3+ hours from South Terminal), 220 km from Isfahan (2.5 hours from Kaveh terminal) with frequent buses.
see: Agha Bozorg Mosque - historical mosque built in the late 18th century with a theological school in its basement
Fin Garden - UNESCO World Heritage Persian Paradise Garden, the oldest garden in Iran (built 1590), covering 2.3 hectares with the historic Fin Bath where Amir-Kabir was murdered
Kashan Bazaar - fantastic mudbrick architecture, with the rooftop offering views over the old city
Timcheh Amin-o-dowleh - renovated caravanserai forming part of the bazaar
Sultan Amir Bath - beautifully restored bathhouse with atmospheric lighting
Boroujerdi House - over a hundred years old, took 18 years to build with exceptional architectural detail
Tabatabaei House - large house with several courtyards, delightful wall paintings and stained glass windows
Ameri-Ha House - traditional Qajar-era house, now a boutique hotel, once one of the largest homes in Persia
Abbasian House - traditional house from the late 18th century with six courtyards and mirror-piece ceilings
Sialk Ziggurat - claimed to be the world's oldest ziggurat, dating to the 3rd millennium BC
Sultan Amir Shrine (Imamzadeh-ye Sultan Mir Ahmad)
Underground City of Ouyi (Noushabad) - labyrinth of tunnels on three underground floors, 10 km north of Kashan
Maranjab Desert Sand Dunes - several square kilometres of sand dunes, 60 km from Kashan
Abyaneh - famous historic village with unique reddish hue, one of the oldest in Iran
wikivoyage.do: Walk the traditional bazaar and neighbouring streets to catch the feeling of an Iranian town on the fringe of the desert. Visit Fin Garden. Take a day trip to Maranjab desert sand dunes and salt lake. Visit the underground city at Noushabad. In spring, visit Qamsar, the biggest rosewater production center in the Middle East. Explore the puppet and toy museum for a change of pace from historical houses.
wikivoyage.eat: Traditional hotels have decent restaurants. Traditional restaurants near the sights and garden restaurants near Fin Garden serve Persian food. Local specialties include Abgoosht-e Kashani (meat and bean stew), Shefteh, Beetroot kofta, and Tass Kebab. The historical bathhouse (Hammam-e-Khan) in the bazaar has been transformed into a traditional coffee shop serving tea and shisha. Carpet and rosewater are the city's most famous products.
wikivoyage.sleep: Ameri-Ha House is a boutique hotel in a spectacular traditional Qajar-era house. Most historical houses in the old district offer an authentic Persian accommodation experience. Traditional hotels provide both atmosphere and convenient access to the major sights. Budget and mid-range options are available around the bazaar area and old town.

name: Kerman

climate.description: Mid-latitude steppe climate with hot arid summers and very cold winters. Large diurnal temperature variations due to high elevation and desert proximity.

wikipedia_summary: Kerman is a city in the Central District of Kerman County, Kerman province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. Located on a sandy plain at 1,749 metres above sea level, Kerman is the largest carpet producing and exporting center in Iran, and a major producer of pistachios on the world market. The city had a population of 537,718 people (2016 census). Kerman province is the largest of Iran's 31 provinces, encompassing nearly 11% of the country's land area, and serves as a gateway to the vast Lut Desert, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

wikivoyage.understand: Kerman is a city with 677,650 inhabitants (2006 census), situated on a sandy plain at 1,749 m above sea level. Kerman is the largest carpet producing and exporting center in Iran, and a large producer of pistachios on the world market.
wikivoyage.get_in: By plane: Ayatollah Hashemi Rafsanjani Airport (KER) has regular flights to Tehran (5-6 daily), Mashhad, Kish Island, Ahvaz, Isfahan, Tabriz, Shiraz, Zahedan, Bandar Abbas, Dubai, Najaf, and Karbala. By bus: Well connected to most parts of the country; taxi from bus station to city maximum 70,000 rials. By car: Freeways connect to Yazd (300 km), Zahedan, Bandar Abbas (600 km), Birjand, Shiraz (600 km), and Mashhad (1,200 km). By train: Connected to Yazd and Zahedan via the national railway network.
see: Kerman Bazaar - stretching 1,200 m from Arg Sq to Moshtag Sq, one of the oldest trading centres in Iran with four smaller bazaars and 20+ branches
Ganjali Khan Square & Complex - similar to Naghshe Jahan, with bazaars, bathhouse, mosque, school, and coin museum (Zarabkhaneh)
Gonbad-e Jabaliyeh (Rock Dome) - historical stone and brick structure inspired by Sassanid period architecture
Sanati Museum - art museum in a Qajar-era building with paintings, sculptures, and a bronze hand by Auguste Rodin
Mouyedi Ice-House - historical ice storage facility
Jameh Mosque - the Friday mosque with concealed entrance from the bazaar
Malek Mosque (Imam Mosque) - a gem of a mosque
Museum of Zoroastrianism and Temple - displays Zoroastrianism customs and objects
Rayen Castle - magnificent old citadel similar to Bam, well-preserved (day trip)
Shazdeh's Garden (Mahan) - UNESCO World Heritage Site, a fine Persian garden 30 km from Kerman
Shah Nimatullah Wali Shrine - in Mahan
Kaluts (Shahdad Desert) - stunning natural sandstone formations in the Lut Desert
Fathabad Garden - historical Persian garden near Kerman
wikivoyage.do: Attend the Sadeh Celebration, an ancient Iranian festival dating to the first Persian Empire, celebrating 50 days before Nowruz. Visit the Kaluts desert formations for a unique natural experience. Explore the living bazaar culture at Ganjali Khan Complex.
wikivoyage.eat: Restaurants (both fast food and traditional) are found around the city, notably on Jomhuri Boulevard. Vakil Traditional Tea House is located in the historic bathhouse inside the bazaar. Qal'eh Restaurant and Teahouse serves tea, snacks, and shisha. Local specialties include Lapeh polo with cumin, Kolompeh (walnut and date pie), and Faloode-e-Kermani (rosewater sorbet). Kerman is famous for its Doogh (yogurt drink) and aromatic distillates.
wikivoyage.sleep: Options include Pars Hotel on Jomhuri Boulevard, Akhavann Hotel on Shahid Sodooghi Avenue, Govashir Hotel, and budget Hotel Omid. Motevalli Bashi Traditional Hotel in Mahan offers single rooms from 300,000 rials with free Wi-Fi and breakfast, but is 40 km outside town.

name: Kermanshah

climate.description: Mediterranean climate with hot summers and very cold winters. The city receives the most rainfall in western Iran, concentrated in winter and early spring months. Summers are dry and sweltering.

wikipedia_summary: Kermanshah is a city in the Central District of Kermanshah province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. The city is 525 kilometres from Tehran in the western part of the country. The 2016 National Census measured the population at 946,651. Kermanshah is widely recognised as the largest Kurdish-populated city in Iran and one of the most significant urban centres of Kurdish culture in the region. The Kurdish dialect of the city, known as Kermashani, belongs to the Southern Kurdish branch of the Kurdish language family. The region is home to the Behistun Inscription, a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring multi-lingual cuneiform inscriptions ordered by Darius the Great, and Taq-e Bostan, a series of large rock reliefs from the Sassanid era.

wikivoyage.understand: Kermanshah is a city in Central Western Iran with a mild climate and many natural and historical sightseeings. Kermanshah has a large population, and Kurdish language as well as some dialects of Kurdish is widely used in the city.
wikivoyage.get_in: By plane: Kermanshah has an international airport with connections to Tehran, Mashhad, and regular flights from Istanbul. By car: Snapp and Tapsi ride-hailing apps work in the city (equivalent to Uber). By train: Kermanshah railway station opened in March 2018 with twice-weekly direct trains to Tehran and Mashhad. By bus: Regular buses from Tehran and other major cities, plus daily bus groups going to Karbala through Baghdad in Iraq.
see: Taq-e Bostan - impressive rock reliefs featuring gigantic equestrian figure of Sassanid king Khosrau II in full battle armor, with carved tree of life patterns and winged angels

Behistun Inscription - UNESCO World Heritage Site, multi-lingual cuneiform inscription on Mount Behistun ordered by Darius I, crucial in deciphering cuneiform script (equivalent of the Rosetta Stone for cuneiform)
Mo'avenalmolk Mosque (Tekyeh Moaven al-molk) - unique Shia mourning site with exclusive tilings picturing Islamic-era warriors, the Battle of Karbala, and Achaemenid kings
Kouhestan Park - near Taq Bostan Mountains with beautiful views of the city
Zagros Paleolithic Museum - first museum in Iran devoted to the Paleolithic period, with stone tools and fossil bones
Museum of Traditional Martial Art - contains wax models of traditional wrestlers
Museum of Epigraphy and Qajar Hand Writings at Tekieh Biglar Baigi
Talab-e Hashilan - 1,500-hectare marsh with floating islands and 200 bird species, 32 km from Kermanshah
Prav Cave - the deepest cave in Asia, located in the Prav Mountains between Taq Bostan and Bisotoon
Qoori Qal'eh Cave - 12 km cave with ever-flowing stream, 92 km from city toward Paveh
wikivoyage.do: Explore the UNESCO World Heritage Behistun Inscription site. Visit Taq-e Bostan rock reliefs at sunset for dramatic lighting. Take a day trip to Talab-e Hashilan marsh for birdwatching (200 species). Explore the deep Prav Cave. Visit Qoori Qal'eh cave with its underground streams and colorful columns.
wikivoyage.eat: Local foods include Dandeh Kebab, Tarkhineh, Khoresh-e Khalal, Sib Polo (rice with potato), Ash-e Abbasali, Abgoosht-e Kermanshahi, and Abgoosht-e Baghi. Sour Cherry tea is the famous drink of Kermanshah. For pastries, visit the Nan Berenji Bazaar for traditional rice cookies (Nan-Brenji), Nan-Roghan, and Nan-Shekari. Kermanshahi Butter is a renowned local product.
wikivoyage.sleep: Budget: Kermanshah Oak Hostel offers dorms from 5.50 EUR and private rooms from 17.50 EUR in the heart of traditional Kermanshah, close to the old bazaar. Splurge: Hotel Parsian (5-star) on Taq Bostan Boulevard is the most comfortable hotel in the city. Hotel Jamshid (4-star) is at Taq Bostan Square.

name: Khorramabad
wikipedia_summary: Khorramabad is a city in the Central District of Khorramabad County, Lorestan province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. Situated in a scenic valley surrounded by mountains, the city lies approximately 100 km east of the Iraqi border. Khorramabad is known for its Falak-ol-Aflak Castle, one of the largest fortresses in Iran, dating to the Sassanid era. The city also features numerous waterfalls and natural springs, making it one of the greenest cities in western Iran.

name: Kish

climate.description: Hot desert climate with very hot, humid summers and warm winters. Minimal rainfall concentrated in winter months. The island's Persian Gulf location creates high humidity year-round.

wikipedia_summary: Kish is a 91.5-square-kilometre resort island in Bandar Lengeh County, Hormozgan Province, off the southern coast of Iran in the Persian Gulf. The island constitutes the city of Kish. Owing to its free trade zone status, the island is marketed as a consumers' paradise, offering shopping centers, tourist attractions, and resort hotels. It has an estimated population of around 55,205 residents and about 12 million visitors annually. Often called the 'Pearl of the Persian Gulf', Kish Island is a unique coral island known for its beautiful beaches, duty-free shopping, and relaxed atmosphere compared to mainland Iran. The island has historical significance dating back to ancient times and features underwater archaeological sites.

wikivoyage.understand: Kish Island, often called the Pearl of the Persian Gulf, has an area of 90 km2 (36 sq. miles). It is known for being a beautiful resort in the Middle East and the Persian Gulf, a unique coral island. Kish Island has relaxed laws in comparison to the rest of Iran. Like mainland Iran, the people of Kish are generally friendly and hospitable.
wikivoyage.get_in: Foreign nationals do not need a visa at authorized arrival and departure points on Kish Island, with stays up to 14 days permitted. Kish Airlines operates flights from Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, and Mashhad. There are ferry connections from Bandar Aftab and more frequent ferries from Bandar Charak. Due to sanctions, flights to Kish cannot be found on western flight search websites. Be aware that entering Kish Island makes someone ineligible for USA ESTA.

do: Snorkeling - said to be among the best in the world

Jet-skiing, sailing, fishing, parasailing
Reef walking and coral viewing

Beach walks along gorgeous white sandy coastline
wikivoyage.eat: Kish Island offers a wide selection of restaurants ranging from Persian to International cuisines. Cafe Aquarium in Pardis II Shopping Mall is a popular hangout serving coffee, sandwiches, banana splits, and fresh fruit juice.
wikivoyage.sleep: Accommodation in Kish is slightly more expensive than mainland Iran but not extortionate. The island has a range of villas, hotels, and guest houses. Hotel reservations often must be made through a travel agency in Tehran.

name: Marvdasht
wikipedia_summary: Marvdasht is a city in the Central District of Marvdasht County, Fars province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. The city is the gateway to Persepolis, the ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire (550-330 BCE) and one of Iran's most important UNESCO World Heritage Sites. The nearby Naqsh-e Rostam necropolis contains the tombs of several Achaemenid kings. Marvdasht is an agricultural center in the fertile Marv Dasht plain, one of the most productive agricultural regions in Iran.

name: Mashhad

climate.description: Cold semi-arid climate with hot summers, cold winters, and a Mediterranean-like dry summer precipitation pattern.

wikipedia_summary: Mashhad, historically also known as Mashad, Meshhed, or Meshed, is the second-most-populous city in Iran, located in the north-east of the country about 740 kilometres east of Tehran. In the Central District of Mashhad County, it serves as the capital of Razavi Khorasan province, the county, and the district. It has a population of about 3,400,000 (2016 census), which includes the areas of Mashhad Taman and Torqabeh. A small village by the 9th century known as Sanabad, it was located along the ancient Silk Road. It outgrew surrounding villages and became known by its current name, meaning 'place of martyrdom', in reference to the Imam Reza shrine, where the eighth Shia Imam, Ali al-Rida, is buried. The shrine is the most pilgrimaged site in Islam, visited by nearly 30 million each year. Between 1736 and 1796, Mashhad became the capital of Afsharid Iran, ruled by the dynasty founded by Nader Shah, whose tomb is located in the city.

wikivoyage.understand: Almost everything about Mashhad relates to its role as the holiest city in Iran, even its name (literally: 'burial place of the martyr'). As the capital of Iran's Khorasan province, Mashhad's year-round cool temperatures and fascinating insights into the Shi'ite heartland have much to offer visitors. Mashhad started life as Sanabad, a village pitstop for trade caravans headed towards Turkmenistan. In the ninth century, the eighth Shi'ite Imam Ali Bin Moosa Al Reza was poisoned by the jealous caliph and buried here. His shrine became one of the holiest sites to Shi'ite Muslims. Over the next thousand years, the shrine complex was levelled, ransacked and rebuilt multiple times. Today the shrine is the most important place in the city and in all of Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: By plane: Mashhad International Airport (MHD) is located just south of the city centre, connected to all Iranian cities with airports and internationally to Dubai, Istanbul, Jeddah, Kabul, and other Middle Eastern cities. By train: Railway station is just north of the Holy Shrine area, with several regular trains to Tehran (fastest in 8 hours), night trains to Isfahan (18 hours), and international connections from Turkmenistan via Sarakhs. By bus: Mostly night buses from other major Iranian cities; the Imam Reza Bus Station serves intercity routes.
see: Imam Reza Shrine Complex - the holiest place for all Shia Iranians, with corridors and domes adorned with Persian mirror mosaic, includes the Ghoharshad Mosque (largest mosque in the world by area)
Central Museum - inside the Holy Shrine complex, showing coins, stamps, astronomical instruments, and historical photographs
Carpet Museum - impressive collection of Persian carpets inside the Holy Shrine complex
Nader Shah Park & Mausoleum - monument to the founder of the Afshar dynasty (1736-1747)
Ferdowsi Park & Mausoleum - tomb of the great Persian poet in nearby Tus, 24 km away
Gombade Sabz Mausoleum
Torghabeh - sightseeing and village restaurants
Shandiz - village restaurants and summer resort
Shah Public Bath - built during the Safavid era in 1648, outstanding example of period architecture
wikivoyage.do: Visit the three large bazaars (Bazaar-e Reza, Sara-ye Bazaar-e Reza, Kuwaiti Bazaar) for Khorassan crafts, fur-cloaks, jewelry, perfume, saffron, and religious souvenirs. Mashhad is the saffron centre of the world. Explore the summer resorts at Torghabeh, Torogh, Akhlamad, Zoshk, and Shandiz.
wikivoyage.eat: Iranian cuisine is a highlight. Chello Kebab and Bakhtiari Kebab are very popular. The most advertised restaurant is Padide Shandiz, with numerous other decent restaurants in Shandiz village. Full meals with rice cost US$5-20. Hezardestan Traditional Teahouse is a beautifully decorated subterranean restaurant on Jannat street serving chicken kebab, dizi, ghormeh, and mashed eggplant. Try the local 'ice pack' ice cream, a uniquely Iranian frozen treat available in many flavors.
wikivoyage.sleep: Mashhad attracts huge numbers of visitors, especially during summer months, so advance reservations are essential. Five-star hotels include Pars Hotel, Ghasr Talaee International Hotel, and Pardisan Hotel. Hotels near the Imam Reza Shrine like Khayyam Hotel offer double bedrooms at US$100-120 per night. Budget rooms for rent around the shrine range from US$30-90 per night depending on distance from the shrine.

name: Masuleh
wikipedia_summary: Masuleh is a city in Sardar-e Jangal District of Fuman County, Gilan province, Iran. Masuleh is approximately 60 km southwest of Rasht and 32 km west of Fuman. The village is 1,050 meters above sea level in the Alborz mountain range, near the southern coast of the Caspian Sea. The village itself has a 100-meter difference in elevation, with buildings built in a stepped arrangement where the roof of one house serves as the courtyard of the house above. Its people are Talysh. Masuleh is known for its unique terraced architecture and has been nominated for UNESCO World Heritage status. Vehicles are not permitted in the village due to its steep terrain.

name: Meybod
wikipedia_summary: Meybod is a city in the Central District of Meybod County, Yazd province, Iran, serving as capital of both the county and the district. Meybod is a major desert city and the second most populous city in the province. The city has a history stretching back over 7,000 years and contains numerous historic sites including the Narin Qal'eh (Narin Castle), one of the oldest mud-brick structures in Iran. Meybod is also known for its traditional crafts including pottery (ziloo weaving) and its well-preserved caravanserais and ice houses (yakhchals).

name: Palangan
wikipedia_summary: Palangan (also known as Patagān, Patakān, and Tangān) is a Kurdish village in Kurdistan Province, Iran. The village is situated on the banks of the mouth of Tangivar River into Sirwan River. Its population was 821 in the 2006 census. There is an ancient fort ruin near the village. Residents speak the endangered Hawrami dialect of the Gorani language, and the village dates back to pre-Islamic times. Palangan is known for its stepped architecture built along both sides of a valley, with stone and mud-brick houses creating a dramatic terraced landscape. The village has become a popular destination for cultural tourism.

name: Qeshm

climate.description: Hot desert climate with very hot summers and mild winters. Similar to nearby Bandar Abbas. Humidity is high due to proximity to the Persian Gulf. Rainfall is minimal and concentrated in winter months.

wikipedia_summary: Qeshm is an arrow-shaped Iranian island in the Strait of Hormuz of the Persian Gulf, measuring roughly 1,500 square kilometres in surface area. Separated from the Iranian mainland by the Clarence Strait (Khuran), Qeshm is the largest island in the Persian Gulf. The island is part of Qeshm County, Hormozgan province, Iran. The island comprises 59 towns and villages with a population of 148,993 at the 2016 census. The local population is engaged in fishing, dhow construction, trade, and services. Qeshm is home to the Hara marine forests, the Stars Valley geological formation, and the Portuguese Castle, making it a destination for eco-tourism and cultural exploration. The island has been designated as a UNESCO Global Geopark.

wikivoyage.understand: Qeshm is the largest island in the Persian Gulf, with rocky coastlines that protect a mountainous and often beautiful interior. The coast is dotted with small villages and towns. Qeshm Island is a duty-free zone.
wikivoyage.get_in: Travelers from 180 different nations do not require a visa to visit Qeshm. Americans, Canadians, and Britons may enter without a visa but must submit an application to the airport police office. The simplest way to reach the island is by flight, with domestic flights from Tehran, Isfahan, Mashhad, and Shiraz, or international flights from Dubai operated by Qeshm Air. Ferries depart hourly from Bandar Abbas main harbour (40-60 minute ride, about 6 EUR). A car ferry also leaves regularly from Bander-e-Pol, about 60 km west of Bandar Abbas.

wikivoyage.eat: Iranian cuisine is available in the city centre and the main town of Dargahan with conventional food stalls and restaurants serving Iranian cuisine with local specialties. The most common item is the Iranian variant of the shewerma (Arabic meat sandwich). Kebabs and other Iranian dishes are available.
wikivoyage.sleep: Qeshm International Hotel on Bahman Blvd is the main accommodation option, along with several other hotels and guesthouses on the island.

name: Qom
wikipedia_summary: Qom is a city in the Central District of Qom County, Qom province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. It is the seventh largest metropolis and the seventh largest city in Iran, located 140 km south of Tehran on the banks of the Qom River. Qom is considered one of the holiest cities in Shia Islam and is home to the Fatima Masumeh Shrine, the sister of Imam Reza. The city is the largest center for Shia scholarship in the world and a significant destination for Shia pilgrims. In antiquity, the Qom region was a stronghold of the cult of Anahita, the Iranian goddess of fertility.

wikivoyage.understand: Qom is a holy city between Tehran and Isfahan, 120 km from Tehran. Qom is one of the holiest cities in Iran and the Middle East, and has a very long history. The famous Fatima Masumeh Shrine is a highly respected site. Qom has for a long time been the main city for religious studies in Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: Qom is well-connected to Tehran and Isfahan by bus and train.

name: Rasht

climate.description: Humid subtropical climate with heavy rainfall year-round, especially in autumn. Known as the 'City of Rain' with over 1300mm annual precipitation.

wikipedia_summary: Rasht is a city in the Central District of Rasht County, Gilan province, Iran, serving as the capital of the province, the county, and the district. Known as the "City of Rain", Rasht has, throughout Iran's history, served as a border city, earning the historical nickname "Dar al-Marz Rasht" or "Rasht on the Border". With a population of 679,995 in 2016, it is the most populous city in northern Iran. The city is situated near the Caspian Sea and is renowned for its lush green landscapes, heavy rainfall, and rich culinary traditions. Rasht serves as a major cultural and economic center of the Gilan province, with a distinct Gilaki identity, language, and cuisine that sets it apart from the rest of Iran.

wikivoyage.understand: Rasht is a city in the north of Iran around 40 km from the shore of the Caspian Sea. Rasht is known in Iran for its beauty and its parks. People from all around the country come there to experience much wetter and colder climate than the rest of the country. It is the capital of the northern Iranian province of Gilan. All in all Rasht and its surroundings offer a starkly different experience from southern Iran.
wikivoyage.get_in: Rasht Airport has a few daily flights from Tehran, most departing early morning or late afternoon. There is also a direct flight to Dubai. Buses connect Rasht with Tehran and neighboring towns, with several departures daily costing about 2-4 EUR. The journey from Tehran takes about 6 hours. There are daily trains from Tehran departing at 07:45 and arriving at 13:10, passing through the Alborz mountains and Sefidrood river valley. Shared taxis (Savaris) run along the Caspian coast making Rasht easily accessible from the region.
see: Shahrdari (Municipality Building) - Rasht's most identifiable landmark with colonial style and distinctive whitewashed tower, beautiful when floodlit at night
Rasht Museum - Small but well-presented in a 1930s house with Gilaki lifestyle displays and 3000-year-old terracotta drinking horns
Golsar - High-class district with shops, restaurants, boutiques, and coffee-shops popular with young adults
Gilan Rural Heritage Museum - 18 km south of Rasht with six full homesteads in 150 ha of woodland showcasing local crafts
Traditional Bazaar

Park-e Shahr (City Central Park)
do: Explore the Golsar neighborhood for a modernized area with shops and restaurants
Visit Masouleh historical village - spectacular village built on steep slopes where roads lie on roofs of houses below
Day trip to Bandar-e-Anzali, Iran's main Caspian port with fish markets and motorboat rides through the lagoon
Hike to Rudkhan Castle, a medieval brick and stone castle 50 km southwest through forested trails
wikivoyage.eat: Rasht and the surrounding Caspian towns are famous for walnut-filled koloocheh cookies, doogh (traditional drink), and local specialties including Shami Rashti, Ardabij (Anar Bij), and Baghala ghatogh. The fertile northern soil provides a great variety of food. Traditional breakfast includes cooked cow head.
wikivoyage.sleep: Budget options include cheap mosaferkhanehs (guesthouses) clustered just south of Shahrdari Square off Emam Khomeini Street. Mid-range and higher options are available throughout the city.

name: Sanandaj
wikipedia_summary: Sanandaj (Kurdish: Senneh) is a city in the Central District of Sanandaj County, in the Kurdistan province of Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. With a population of around 412,000, it is the second largest Kurdish city and 20th largest city overall in Iran. The city is considered a historic centre of Kurdish cultural and intellectual life, particularly noted for its tradition of Kurdish music, poetry, and carpet weaving.

name: Sar Agha Seyyed
wikipedia_summary: Sar Agha Seyyed is a remote stepped village in the Zagros Mountains of Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province, Iran. At an elevation of approximately 2,600 meters, the village features a unique terraced architecture similar to Masuleh, where the rooftop of each house serves as the courtyard for the house above. The village is inhabited by Bakhtiari people and maintains a traditional way of life. It has gained attention in recent years as one of Iran's most photogenic and architecturally distinctive villages, often compared to a natural amphitheater carved into the mountainside.

name: Sari
wikipedia_summary: Sari is a city in the Central District of Sari County, Mazandaran province, serving as capital of the district, county, and province. Sari was the former capital of Iran for a short period and is in the north of the country, between the northern slopes of the Alborz Mountains and southern coast of the Caspian Sea. Sari is the largest and most populous city of Mazandaran and ranked as the most livable city in Iran. The city has a rich history dating back thousands of years, with archaeological evidence showing settlement from at least the 5th millennium BCE.

name: Semnan
wikipedia_summary: Semnan is a city in the Central District of Semnan County, Semnan province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. The city is on the alluvial fan of the Golrudbar creek in the north-central part of the country, 216 km east of Tehran and 640 km west of Mashhad. With a population of 185,129 people, Semnan is the hub of the Semnani language, a sub-branch of the Iranian languages spoken to the north. It is home to Semnan University and has a long history as a stopping point on the Silk Road.

name: Shahr-e Kord
wikipedia_summary: Shahr-e Kord is a city in the Central District of Shahrekord County, Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province, Iran, serving as capital of the province, the county, and the district. It is the largest city in the province, located 90 km from Isfahan. At an elevation of 2,070 metres, it is one of the highest provincial capitals in Iran. The city is known for its cold winters and is a center of Bakhtiari Luri culture. The surrounding province features dramatic Zagros mountain scenery and nomadic Bakhtiari tribes.

name: Shiraz
wikipedia_summary: Shiraz is the fifth-most-populous city of Iran and the capital of Fars province, which is historically known by the exonym Persia proper. As of the 2016 national census, the population of the city was 1,565,572 people, and its built-up area with Sadra was home to almost 1,800,000 inhabitants. A census in 2021 showed an increase in the city's population to 1,995,500 people. Shiraz is located in southwestern Iran on the rudkhaneye khoshk (lit. 'dry river') seasonal river. The city has a moderate climate and has been a regional trade center for over a thousand years. The earliest reference to the city, as Tiraziš, is on Elamite clay tablets dated to 2000 BCE. The modern city was founded by the Sasanian dynasty and restored by the Umayyad Caliphate in 693 CE and grew prominent under the successive Iranian Saffarid and Buyid dynasties in the 9th and 10th–11th centuries, respectively. In the 13th century, Shiraz became a leading center of the arts and letters, due to the encouragement of its ruler and the presence of many Persian scholars and artists. Two famous poets of Iran, Hafez and Saadi, are from Shiraz, whose tombs are located on the north side of the current city boundaries.

wikivoyage.understand: Shiraz is the capital city of Fars province and a treasure trove of Persian culture. It was the capital of Iran during the Zand dynasty's era (1747-79), and is the celebrated birthplace of the great Persian poets Hafiz and Saadi. The city has a population of about 1,500,000. In previous centuries, Shiraz was famous for a locally produced wine, although this has no connection to the modern Shiraz (or Syrah) wine, which is of French origin. Shiraz is a city of poets, literature, wine (in the past), and flowers. It is also considered by many Iranians to be the city of gardens, due to the many gardens and fruit trees found throughout the city, such as the famous Eram Garden. The pleasant climate, combined with a rich cultural heritage stretching back millennia, makes Shiraz one of Iran's most popular tourist destinations.

see.name: Arg of Karim Khan
see.description: This former a prison is an architectural wonder on exhibit. The design of the citadel combines military and residential architecture, for it was the home of Karim Khan and the military centre of the dynasty. Tile works depicting legendary tales were added at the entrance gate of the citadel during the Qajar period.

see.name: Shah Cheragh
see.description: A funerary monument and mosque, housing the tomb of Seyed Amir Ahmad, known as Shah-e Cheragh, the brother of Imam Reza, came to Shiraz in the latter half of the 8th century. He died in the city and his tomb is now a place of pilgrimage. The structure, tile work and the dome of the mausoleum have been rebuilt several times over the centuries. The tomb, the beautiful silver doors and the exquisite mirror work are the handicrafts of masters and contemporary artists of Shiraz. Regarded as the holiest place in Shiraz. Besides, it houses a small museum exhibiting about 30 very old and rare Qur'an b
see.price: Free entrance and a free guided tour

see.name: Nasir al-Mulk Mosque
see.description:
Built during the Qājār era, the mosque has extensively colored glass in its façade, and other traditional elements such as panj kāseh-i (five concaves) in its design. One of the most photographed mosques in southern Iran. Visit before 10:00 to see its beautiful mosaic glasses lit up by the background sun light.
| image=Nasr ol Molk mosque2.jpg | wikipedia=Nasir ol Molk Mosque | wikidata=Q1962312

see.name: Jame’e Atigh Mosque
see.description: A 9th-century mosque.

see.name: Vakil Mosque
see.description: Built between 1751 and 1773, during the Zand period; however, it was restored in the 19th century during the Qajar period where its exuberant floral decorative tiles date from. Parts of the mosque is still under construction so it's barely used as a mosque by locals.

see.name: Narenjestan Ghavam museum
see.description: Both traditional and historical house, it was built in the mid-to-late 19th century by Mirza Ibrahim Khan. The Qavam "Naranjestan" preserves the elegance and refinement enjoyed by the upper-class families during the 19th century. The mirrored porch was a focal point of the house, overlooking onto gardens lined with date palms and flowers. The house today is a museum open to the public.

see.name: Khan-e Zinat ol-Molk House
see.description: Historical house and adjoining gardens that used to be connected to Narenjestan Ghavam by underground tunnel. Now it works as a separate museum which besides of the traditional Iranian house houses Fars History museum in its basement.

see.name: Sayyed Alaeddin Hossein Mosque
see.description: It holds shrine of Sayyed Alaeddin Hossein, one of the sons of the seventh imam. Mosque's walls and ceilings are covered with intricate mirror mosaics and coloured glass.
see.price: Free entrance and free guided tour

see.name: Pars Museum
see.description: Museum's pavilion was the place in which royal guests were hosted during the Zand dynasty. It was also used for holding official ceremonies and later became the burial place of Karim Khan Zand. Now it exhibits 30 handwritten Qurans and paintings of Persian artists.

see.name: Khan school
see.description: It's been fully reconstructed where only its facade belongs to the original structure.

see.name: Armenian Church
see.description: 17th-century Armenian church, which survived from the Safavid era.

see.name: Bazaar-e Vakil
see.description:
Huge ancient bazaar specializing in fine carpets, textiles, antiques and handicrafts, and spices. There is also a wonderful courtyard with a pool located in the bazaar, perfect for photographs. Caravansarais, bath houses, and old shops where hundreds of vendors are housed.
| image=Bazaar de Vakil, Shiraz, Irán, 2016-09-24, DD 48.jpg | wikipedia=Vakil Bazaar | wikidata=Q846326

see.name: Saray-e-Moshir
see.description: Caravansary at the south entrance of Bazaar Vakil, which now functions as exhibition space for Iranian handicrafts.

see.name: Vakil Bath
see.description: An old public bath which has been remodelled into a museum of waxworks.

see.name: Hafez Tomb
see.description: Mausoleum of Hafez (1324–1391), the greatest master of Persian lyric poetry and the literary giant of the 14th century in the west and central Asia, was born in Shiraz, lived all his life here, sang its praises in unsurpassed verse and was buried in a garden known after him as the Hafezieh, in the northeast part of the city. The wide appeal of this poet among all Persian-speaking people make his tomb a cherished placed, visited by all. This mausoleum was rebuilt in the early 1950s. A flight of stone steps reaches to the tomb under a tiled cupola resembling a dervish’s hat. The tombstone is bea

see.name: Saadi Tomb
see.description: Here lie the earthly remains of one of Iran’s greatest poets. Even from the very early days after the poet’s death, the mausoleum of Sa’di became a place of pilgrimage to lovers of poetry and literature. In 1808 AD Karim Khan Zand renovated the mausoleum. The tomb was rebuilt in the early 1950s. The porch with its tall columns of pinkish marble is a traditional feature of Iranian architecture.

see.name: Ali Ibn Hamzeh Holly Shrine
see.description: Built over the shrine of Emir Ali, a nephew of Shah Cheragh. The building itself is quite new as it had to be rebuilt after an earthquake. However, it can boast of the richly-ornamented interiors, parts of which are made of marble and mirror mosaics.
see.price: Free entrance and free guided tour

see.name: Shah Shoja Tomb
see.description: Tomb of Shah Shoja', the Mozafarid emir of Persia, and patron of Hafiz.

see.name: Baba Kuhi Tomb
see.description: The Tomb of Baba Kuhi sits atop a mountain overlooking the city.

see.name: Qur'an Gate
see.description: The city's main entrance. The original gate was built as an ornamental decoration by the Buwwayhids (Buwayhid dynasty) about 1000 years ago, but this was replaced 60 years ago by new gate, which is considered one of the finest architectural designs in Iran and has won numerous awards. From the gate walk up the stone stairways to enjoy picturesque and panoramic views of Shiraz. The tomb of Khajooyeh Kermanee, a famous poet, is also located here (entrance ticket 115,000 rials). Hidden in the alleys on the hill-side are numerous restaurants that serve the finest chelo kabob. It is an excellent pl

see.name: Haft Tanan Museum
see.description: It's a mausoleum, where seven Sufi mystics are buried. Besides, there is a museum of stones that were collected from historical sites in Fars province.

see.name: Gahvareh Did
see.description: People say this is the place where a guard tower was located in the old days, nowadays it has a stone replica of it. Besides, it's a fantastic observation point. Be prepared to climb up 800 steps to get there. Or be lucky that one of the guards at the gate will drive you up the hill. Even further up the road you'll end up at Mortaz Ali well, where you'll have an even more impressive overview of Shiraz (if there is no smog).

see.name: Qasr e Abu Nasr
see.name_local: Abu-Nasr Palace or Takht e Sulayman [Throne of Solomon]
see.description: Fortress built in Parthian Empire and was important and strategic location in Sassanid Empire. Archaeologists found various drachmas and art crafts belonged to different historical periods there. There is almost nothing left from those times, just stones.

do.name: Delgosha Garden
do.description: Enjoy a walk in this lovely old garden with flowers and bitter orange trees.

do.name: Afif abad Garden
do.description: Garden and houses owned by the Ghavami family. It contains a former royal mansion, a historical weapons museum, and a Persian garden that is one of the oldest gardens in Shiraz, all open to the public.

do.name: Eram Garden
do.description: Inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this stunningly beautiful complex contains a vast network of gardens, a colorful palace, and a system of small artificial rivers flowing throughout the entire area. It houses wonderful flora, and you can follow the little canals' intricate system. Make sure the weather is sunny before coming here!

do.name: Jahan Nama Garden
do.price: 115,000 rials plus separate ticket (20,000 rials) is required for the garden's museum

eat.name: Moshit Restaurant
eat.description: A small restaurant that also serves breakfast: fried eggs, tea, and the famous Ash-e-shiraz.

eat.description: Ice cream shop, more creamy than most other ice cream found in Shiraz. Try the special cone ice cream. The shop is popular with the locals, so expect a queue. No seats but there is a small park beside.

wikivoyage.eat_overview:
Kebab with rice - Like elsewhere in Iran, the mighty old kebab with rice is a common fare, though Shiraz does offer a local variation on the national dish.
Shirazi salad - A delicious and famous salad available throughout Iran, made with cucumbers, tomatoes, onions and a simple lemon juice and olive oil dressing.
Kalam polo - Traditional food cooked only in Shiraz. Some delivery food restaurants serve this food, but it can hardly be found in normal restaurants. It is made of rice, meatballs, chopped cabbage and some other vegetables and spices.
Ash-e Sabzi - Ash-e Sabzi or Ash-e Shirazi is a kind of soup made of vegetables and meat. It is served all over the city, but mainly early in the morning, 05:00-07:30.
Masghati Halva - A great dessert and snack.
Nokhod (Chickpea) Cookie - Delicious cookie made of chickpeas, it makes for great souvenirs.
Shirazi Faloodeh - Paloodeh/Faloodeh is an ice cream made of rice starch, which takes the form of noodles. It is flavored with "sharbat" (sugar water), which can be of different flavors. Shirazi paloodeh is the best of its kind, especially when mixed with Bahar-e-Naranj Distill.

Many of these places have live music playing.

Lotus Restaurant, on the top floor of Setareh-e-Fars Shopping Centre
